decisions.decision: Drill-down should be a side drawer (desktop) + full sub-page or bottom-sheet (mobile), NOT a centered modal
decisions.rationale: User chose this. Research confirmed Whoop/Oura/Apple/Garmin all use full-page navigation or drawers, not modals, for primary domains. Modals compete with the report flow.

decisions.decision: Build the comparison as a static HTML file at mockups/pillar-options.html using Tailwind CDN
decisions.rationale: User explicitly asked to see all three side-by-side in a new HTML file before picking

decisions.decision: Quick task 260509-ody is considered complete and tracked in STATE.md, even though the modal it built is being reconsidered
decisions.rationale: The clickable-pillar wiring + state mgmt + drawer-style mount-point lands cleanly; only the modal visual is being replaced

decisions.decision: Admin pages (/portal/admin/users, invitations, pillars, audit-logs, prescriptions) all use a clickable Administration breadcrumb link to /portal/admin
decisions.rationale: User asked for breadcrumbs to go back. Audit-logs additionally moved from /admin/audit-logs into /portal/admin/audit-logs so it inherits the portal sidebar.

next_action: Wait for user to pick Option 1, 2, or 3 from mockups/pillar-options.html. Once chosen, rewrite src/components/report/PillarsDisplay.tsx to that style and replace PillarsDisplayModal.tsx with a side drawer (desktop right slide-in + mobile full-page or bottom-sheet).
context_notes: Mid-redesign of the Section 11 'Peak Living Pillars' visual. Multiple iterations: HUD digital columns -> Tailwind bubbly -> user said 'looks bad' -> research -> mockup HTML with 3 options + shared drawer. Current src/components/report/PillarsDisplay.tsx is the bubbly Tailwind version (commit 0629cc0). The user wants a side drawer for drill-down (not a modal) regardless of which pillar style wins. The existing PillarsDisplayModal.tsx is the section-local modal built by quick task 260509-ody — its data wiring (markerToPillar grouping, tier buckets, marker rows) can be reused when building the drawer; only the chrome changes.
